Section.Text: The information banks that may be searched under this Part are the information banks designated by the regulations from among the information banks controlled by the Department of Human Resources Development, the Canada Revenue Agency and the Canada Employment Insurance Commission.
Subsection 6(2) of the Feeds Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The President of the Canada Border Services Agency may designate inspectors under paragraph 9(2)(b) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act for the purposes of enforcing this Act.

Inspectors shall be given certificates in a form established by the President of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency or the President of the Canada Border Services Agency, as the case may be, attesting to their designation and, on entering any place under subsection 7(1), an inspector shall, if so required, produce the certificate to the person in charge of that place.

Section.Text: Subsection 6(2) of the Fertilizers Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The President of the Canada Border Services Agency may designate inspectors under paragraph 9(2)(b) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act for the purposes of enforcing this Act.

Inspectors shall be given certificates in a form established by the President of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency or the President of the Canada Border Services Agency, as the case may be, attesting to their designation and, on entering any place under subsection 7(1), an inspector shall, if so required, produce the certificate to the person in charge of that place.

Section.Text: Schedule I.1 to the Financial Administration Act is amended by striking out, in column I, the reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence des services frontaliers du Canada

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and the corresponding reference in column II to the Appropriate Minister for that Agency.

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence des services frontaliers du Canada

Section.Text: Subsection 17(2) of the Fish Inspection Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The President of the Canada Border Services Agency may designate inspectors under paragraph 9(2)(b) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act for the purposes of enforcing this Act.

Inspectors shall be given certificates in a form established by the President of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency or the President of the Canada Border Services Agency, as the case may be, attesting to their designation and, on entering any place under subsection 4(1), an inspector shall, if so required, produce the certificate to the person in charge of that place.

Section.Text: Subsection 32(2) of the Health of Animals Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The President of the Canada Border Services Agency may designate inspectors under paragraph 9(2)(b) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act for the purposes of enforcing this Act.

Inspectors, officers and veterinary inspectors shall be given certificates in a form established by the President of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency or the President of the Canada Border Services Agency, as the case may be, attesting to their designation and, on entering any place under this Act, an inspector, officer or veterinary inspector shall show the certificate to the person in charge of the place if the person requests proof of the designation.

Section.Text: Section 4 of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (2), the Minister of Citizenship and Immigration is responsible for the administration of this Act.
The Minister as defined in section 2 of the Canada Border Services Agency Act is responsible for the administration of this Act as it relates to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: examinations at ports of entry;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the enforcement of this Act, including arrest, detention and removal;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the establishment of policies respecting the enforcement of this Act and inadmissibility on grounds of security, organized criminality or violating human or international rights; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: determinations under any of subsections 34(2), 35(2) and 37(2).

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (1) and (2), the Governor in Council may specify

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: which Minister referred to in subsections (1) and (2) shall be the Minister for the purposes of any provision of this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that both Ministers may be the Minister for the purposes of any provision of this Act and the circumstances under which each Minister shall be the Minister.

Subsection.Text: Any order made under subsection (3) must be published in Part II of the Canada Gazette.
Paragraphs 150.1(1)(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the collection, retention, use, disclosure and disposal of information for the purposes of this Act or for the purposes of program legislation as defined in section 2 of the Canada Border Services Agency Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure of information for the purposes of national security, the defence of Canada or the conduct of international affairs, including the implementation of an agreement or arrangement entered into under section 5 of the Department of Citizenship and Immigration Act or section 13 of the Canada Border Services Agency Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 150.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:
Regulations made under subsection (1) may include conditions under which the collection, retention, use, disposal and disclosure may be made.

Section.Text: Subsection 244(13) of the Income Tax Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Every document purporting to have been executed under, or in the course of the administration or enforcement of, this Act over the name in writing of the Minister, the Deputy Minister of National Revenue, the Commissioner of Customs and Revenue, the Commissioner of Revenue or an officer authorized to exercise a power or perform a duty of the Minister under this Act is deemed to have been signed, made and issued by the Minister, the Deputy Minister, the Commissioner of Customs and Revenue, the Commissioner of Revenue or the officer unless it has been called in question by the Minister or by a person acting for the Minister or Her Majesty.

Section.Text: The definition inspector in subsection 2(1) of the Meat Inspection Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: inspector means a person designated as an inspector pursuant to subsection 12(1) or (1.1);

Section.Text: Subsection 12(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The President of the Canada Border Services Agency may designate inspectors under paragraph 9(2)(b) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act for the purposes of enforcing this Act.

Inspectors shall be given certificates in a form established by the President of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency or the President of the Canada Border Services Agency, as the case may be, attesting to their designation and, on entering any place or vehicle referred to in subsection 13(1), an inspector shall, if so required, produce the certificate to the person in charge of that place or vehicle.

Section.Text: Subsection 21(2) of the Plant Protection Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The President of the Canada Border Services Agency may designate inspectors under paragraph 9(2)(b) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act for the purposes of enforcing this Act.

Inspectors shall be given certificates in a form established by the President of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency or the President of the Canada Border Services Agency, as the case may be, attesting to their designation and, on entering any place under this Act, an inspector shall show the certificate to the person in charge of the place if the person requests proof of the inspector’s designation.

Heading.TitleText: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act

Subsection.Text: The definition Commissioner in section 2 of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act is repealed.
The definition Minister in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Minister means, in relation to sections 25 to 39, the Solicitor General of Canada and, in relation to any other provision of this Act, the member of the Queen’s Privy Council for Canada who is designated by the Governor in Council as the Minister for the purposes of that provision.

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Act is amended by adding the following definition in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: President means the President of the Canada Border Services Agency appointed under subsection 7(1) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 38(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: information set out in reports made under subsection 12(1) in respect of currency or monetary instruments imported into Canada from that state will be provided to a department, institution or agency of that state that has powers and duties similar to those of the Canada Border Services Agency in respect of the reporting of currency or monetary instruments; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 55(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Revenue Agency, if the Centre also determines that the information is relevant to an offence of evading or attempting to evade paying taxes or duties imposed under an Act of Parliament administered by the Minister of National Revenue;

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Border Services Agency, if the Centre also determines that the information is relevant to an offence of evading or attempting to evade paying taxes or duties imposed under an Act of Parliament administered by the Agency; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 55(3)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Canada Border Services Agency, if the Centre also determines that the information would promote the objective set out in paragraph 3(1)(i) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act and is relevant to determining whether a person is a person described in sections 34 to 42 of that Act or to an offence under any of sections 117 to 119, 126 or 127 of that Act.

Subsection.Text: Section 55 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):
Paragraph (3)(b) or (b.1) does not apply in respect of an offence relating to taxes or duties imposed under a prescribed Act or a prescribed portion of an Act.

Section.Text: Every reference to Commissioner or Commissioner’s in the following provisions of the Act is replaced by a reference to President or President’s, respectively:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: section 20;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 26(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 31;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 32(3) and (4);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 35(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 39(2).

Section.Text: Part I of Schedule I to the Public Service Superannuation Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence des services frontaliers du Canada

Section.Text: The definition Minister in section 2 of the Royal Canadian Mint Act is repealed.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 2:
The Governor in Council may designate a member of the Queen’s Privy Council for Canada to be the Minister for the purposes of this Act.
Subsection 5(2) of the Seeds Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The President of the Canada Border Services Agency may designate inspectors under paragraph 9(2)(b) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act for the purposes of enforcing this Act.

Inspectors shall be given certificates in a form established by the President of the Canadian Food Inspection Agency or the President of the Canada Border Services Agency, as the case may be, attesting to their designation and, on entering any place under subsection 6(1), an inspector shall show the certificate to the person in charge of the place if the person requests proof of the inspector’s designation.
The definition Commissioner in subsection 2(1) of the Special Import Measures Act is repealed.
The definition Minister in subsection 2(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Minister means the Solicitor General of Canada;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: President means the President of the Canada Border Services Agency appointed under subsection 7(1) of the Canada Border Services Agency Act;

Section.Text: Section 94 of the Act is replaced by the following:

A ruling given by the Tribunal on the question of who is the importer in Canada of any goods imported or to be imported into Canada is binding on the President, and on every person employed by the Canada Border Services Agency in the administration or enforcement of this Act, with respect to the particular goods in relation to which the ruling is given, unless the Tribunal is fraudulently misled or, in the case only of goods to be imported into Canada, material facts that are not available to the President at the time the Tribunal gives its ruling come to the President’s attention after it is given.

Subparagraph.Text: Part II of Schedule I; and

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: Part I of Schedule I to the Public Service Superannuation Act.

Section.Text: Every reference to the “Canada Customs and Revenue Agency” in the following provisions is replaced by a reference to the “Canada Border Services Agency”:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 4.81(4)(b) of the Aeronautics Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 44 of the Copyright Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 42.1 of the Firearms Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph 38(1)(b) of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act.

Section.Text: Every reference to the “Commissioner of Customs and Revenue” in the following provisions is replaced by a reference to the “Commissioner of Revenue”:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the Canada Customs and Revenue Agency Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition Commissioner in section 2, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 462.48(3) of the Criminal Code before paragraph (c);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 97(1) of the Employment Insurance Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: section 63 of the Energy Administration Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the Income Tax Act,

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the Petroleum and Gas Revenue Act,

Section.Text: Every reference to the “Commissioner of Customs and Revenue” in the following provisions of the Customs Tariff is replaced by a reference to the “President of the Canada Border Services Agency”:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 134(1) and (2); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule.

Section.Text: Every reference to the “Minister of National Revenue” in the following provisions is replaced by a reference to the “Solicitor General of Canada”:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the Aeronautics Act,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 40(1.1) of the Canada Post Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the Coasting Trade Act,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the definition “Minister” in section 44.1 of the Copyright Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the Customs Tariff,

Subparagraph.Label: (xii)
Subparagraph.Text: the portion of section 99 before paragraph (a),

Subparagraph.Label: (xviii)
Subparagraph.Text: the portion of section 108 before paragraph (a),

Subparagraph.Label: (xxiii)
Subparagraph.Text: the portion of subsection 113(4) before paragraph (a),

Subparagraph.Label: (xxxiii)
Subparagraph.Text: the List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: section 52 of the Firearms Act;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 16(1) of the Department of Industry Act;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: in the Canada Shipping Act,

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: section 25 of the Statistics Act;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the definition “Minister” in section 52 of the Trade Marks Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: section 24 of the Visiting Forces Act.

Section.Text: The reference to the “Minister of National Revenue” in paragraph (a) of the definition “prescribed” in subsection 2(1) of the English version of the Customs Tariff is replaced by a reference to the “Solicitor General of Canada”.

Subsection.Text: If the definition employee in subsection 2(1) of the Public Service Labour Relations Act, as enacted by section 2 of the Public Service Modernization Act, comes into force before section 1 of this Act comes into force, then, on the day on which section 1 of this Act comes into force, subparagraph 138(q)(i) of this Act is repealed.

If the definition managerial or confidential position in subsection 2(1) of the Public Service Labour Relations Act, as enacted by section 2 of the Public Service Modernization Act, comes into force before section 1 of this Act comes into force, then, on the day on which section 1 of this Act comes into force, subparagraph 138(q)(ii) of this Act is repealed.

If section 11 of the Public Service Modernization Act comes into force before section 1 of this Act comes into force, then, on the day on which section 1 of this Act comes into force, subparagraph 138(q)(iii) of this Act is repealed.
On the later of the coming into force of section 11 of the Public Service Modernization Act and the coming into force of section 1 of this Act,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the reference in Schedule V to the Financial Administration Act to the “Canada Customs and Revenue Agency” is replaced by a reference to the “Canada Revenue Agency”; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: Schedule IV to the Financial Administration Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Agence des services frontaliers du Canada

Subsection.Text: If section 1 of this Act comes into force before subsection 49(1) of the Public Service Modernization Act, then, on the day on which section 1 of this Act comes into force, that subsection is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of the new Act, including any application under section 58 of the new Act, an employee who, on or after the day on which the definition “managerial or confidential position” in subsection 2(1) of that Act comes into force, is employed as a legal officer in the Department of Justice or the Canada Revenue Agency is deemed not to be included in any unit determined, in accordance with the former Act, to constitute a unit of employees appropriate for collective bargaining.

If section 1 of this Act comes into force before section 95 of the Public Service Modernization Act, then, on the day on which section 1 of this Act comes into force, that section and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Paragraph 16(2)(c) of the English version of the Canada Revenue Agency Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is employed on a full-time basis in the federal public administration or the public service of a province or territory.

Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 224 of the Public Service Modernization Act and the coming into force of section 1 of this Act, subsection 10(3) of the English version of this Act is replaced by the following:

The President and the Executive Vice-president are deemed to be employed in the public service for the purposes of the Public Service Superannuation Act and to be employed in the federal public administration for the purposes of the Government Employees Compensation Act and any regulations made under section 9 of the Aeronautics Act.

On the later of the coming into force of section 224 of the Public Service Modernization Act and the coming into force of section 1 of this Act, a reference to the “public service of Canada” in the following provisions of the English version of this Act is replaced by a reference to “federal public administration”:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition former agency in section 16; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 18(1) and (2).

Subsection.Text: If section 1 of this Act comes into force before section 224 of the Public Service Modernization Act, then, on the day on which section 1 of this Act comes into force, paragraph 224(k) of that Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: sections 21 and 66 of the Canada Revenue Agency Act;

Subsection.Text: If section 1 of this Act comes into force before section 229 of the Public Service Modernization Act, then, on the day on which section 1 of this Act comes into force, that section and the heading before it are replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Subsection 55(1) of the Canada Revenue Agency Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of deployments or appointments made, or advertised internal appointment processes, under the Public Service Employment Act, employees of the Agency must be treated as if they were employees within the meaning of the Public Service Employment Act and had the rights of recourse provided by that Act.

Subsections (2) and (3) apply if Bill C-6, introduced in the 1st Session of the 38th Parliament and entitled the Department of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness Act (in this section, the “other Act”), receives royal assent.

On the later of the coming into force of section 1 of this Act and section 1 of the other Act, every reference to the “Solicitor General of Canada” in the following provisions is replaced by a reference to the “Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness”:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in section 2 of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: paragraph (b) of the definition Minister in section 2 of the Agriculture and Agri-Food Administrative Monetary Penalties Act, as enacted by section 30 of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: section 5 of the Cultural Property Import and Export Act, as enacted by section 59 of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in the Customs Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the definition Minister in subsection 2(1), as enacted by subsection 60(2) of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 97.211(1), as enacted by section 76 of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 97.22(3), as enacted by section 77 of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 97.34(2), as enacted by subsection 78(1) of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: subsections 97.34(4) and (5), as enacted by subsection 78(2) of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 107(3)(b), as enacted by subsection 80(2) of this Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 97.22(2) and sections 97.23 and 97.27, as amended by section 84 of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in the Customs Tariff,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 108(c), as enacted by section 88 of this Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the provisions referred to in section 89 of this Act, as amended by that section;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: in the Excise Act, 2001,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 9(3), as enacted by section 93 of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 68(1), as enacted by subsection 94(1) of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsections 68(3) and (4), as enacted by subsection 94(2) of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: subparagraph 188(7)(b)(ii), as enacted by subsection 95(2) of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 189(4), as enacted by section 96 of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph (a.1) of the definition confidential information in subsection 211(1), as enacted by section 97 of this Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 301(8.1), as enacted by section 98 of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: in the Excise Tax Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 70(2.1) as enacted by section 101 of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 105(5.1), as enacted by subsection 102(1) of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 106.1(1.1), as enacted by section 103 of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 215.1(2)(b), as enacted by subsection 105(1) of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: paragraph 215.1(3)(b), as enacted by subsection 105(2) of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: subsections 335(5.1) and (8.1), as enacted by section 107 of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 1 of Part X of Schedule VI, as enacted by section 108 of this Act,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 4 of Part I of Schedule X, as enacted by section 109 of this Act, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: section 6 of Part I of Schedule X, as enacted by section 110 of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in section 2 of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act, as enacted by subsection 124(2) of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the definition Minister in subsection 2(1) of the Special Import Measures Act, as enacted by subsection 132(2) of this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions referred to in section 142 of this Act, as amended by that section.

Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of section 1 of this Act and section 1 of the other Act, the reference to the “Solicitor General of Canada” in paragraph (a) of the definition “prescribed” in subsection 2(1) of the English version of the Customs Tariff, as amended by section 143 of this Act, is replaced by a reference to the “Minister of Public Safety and Emergency Preparedness”.

On the later of the coming into force of section 1 of this Act and section 1 of the other Act, every reference to the “solliciteur général du Canada” in the French version of the provisions referred to in section 89 is replaced by a reference to “ministre de la Sécurité publique et de la Protection civile”.
If it comes into force after section 114 of this Act, paragraph 34(1)(m) of the other Act is repealed on its coming into force.

Section.Text: If Bill C-22, introduced in the 1st Session of the 38th Parliament and entitled the Department of Social Development Act (in this section, the “other Act”), receives royal assent, then, on the later of the coming into force of section 1 of this Act and section 2 of the other Act, section 15 of the Family Orders and Agreements Enforcement Assistance Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Information banks that may be searched
Section.Text: The information banks that may be searched under this Part are the information banks designated by the regulations from among the information banks controlled by the Department of Social Development, the Canada Revenue Agency and the Canada Employment Insurance Commission.
This Act, except for sections 144 to 146, comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
